figures.caption: Trie over the ten-word vocabulary. Nodes are prefixes; double circles are complete words. Edges are labeled by the consumed character.
Minimized deterministic finite automaton (DFA) for the same vocabulary. Each state lists the vocabulary words still consistent with the prefix read since the last space.

Training loss (blue, 51-iteration rolling median of per-window cross-entropy) and stochastic word-error rate (orange, right axis: percent of space-delimited tokens not in the vocabulary during long sampled rollouts).

Three 50-character rows: excerpt from the training corpus (top), stochastic sample at initialization (middle), stochastic sample after training (bottom). Green/red per-character coloring marks in-vocabulary vs out-of-vocabulary segments in the generated rows.
Learned weight matrices after training. Left: input weights $W_{xh}$ (character columns $\times$ hidden rows). Right: recurrent weights $W_{hh}$ (hidden $\times$ hidden).
Random init vs learned input and recurrent weights.
Hierarchically clustered recurrent weights $W_{hh}$.
Hierarchically clustered input weights $W_{xh}$.
Block coupling, cluster cohesion, and input-tuning entropy.
Init vs final feedforward balance metrics.
Softmax next-character probabilities at every timestep (columns), with the true next character highlighted. Brighter cells are higher predicted probability.

For each input character, all timesteps where that character was read. Columns are labeled by **in-word prefix** (e.g. `h`, `ha`, `hat` after a space). Rows are hidden units; columns are occurrences, optionally clustered by activation similarity.
One panel per vocabulary character, showing $P(\text{next}=c\mid\mathbf{h})$ over the PCA plane (from softmax on 2D-reconstructed $\mathbf{h}$).
Same correlation matrix, but timesteps are **grouped by DFA state** (all states shown). Diagonal blocks are within-state correlations; off-diagonal blocks are between-state.
Hidden activations over a 50-character analysis window. Rows are hidden units $h_0\ldots h_{31}$; columns are timesteps (input characters shown along the bottom).
Hierarchically clustered heatmap of all timesteps $\times$ hidden units in the analysis window. Row and column dendrograms group similar timesteps; tick labels are in-word prefixes.
Four 2D embeddings of the same 50 hidden states: PCA, UMAP, t-SNE, and Isomap. Points are annotated with in-word prefix labels; colors follow embedding-specific layout.
Left: minimized DFA from Figure 2. Right: PCA of hidden states with point color = DFA state and text label = in-word prefix. Leader lines connect grouped prefix annotations.
PCA plane with 2D-reconstructed hidden states. Left: argmax next-character label in each region. Right: prediction entropy (nats). Overlaid points carry prefix annotations.
PCA trajectories from one space timestep to the next (space-to-space segments) in the 50-character window. Each word path is colored by word identity; faint background shows optional no-input flow.
Pearson correlation matrix between hidden vectors at all pairs of timesteps, with hierarchical clustering on rows/columns. Tick labels: in-word prefix; label color = minimized DFA state.

Pairwise Euclidean distances between hidden vectors (subsampled pairs). Within/between splits for in-word prefix (excluding current char), full string (prefix + char), DFA state, word position, and character; plus all pairs.

Six-panel comparison of feature separation metrics (centroid gap, silhouette, multivariate η², pairwise within/between medians, shuffle z-scores, ratio p-values) across lexical features on prefix-condensed hidden states.
Linear decoding of DFA state, character, and position from hidden states.
Decoding curves across training seeds (realizability).
Hidden-unit selectivity for DFA state, character, and position.
Learning curves across word-length conditions.
Feature separation across word-length conditions.
